name: network-101
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "set up a web server", "configure HTTP or HTTPS", "perform SNMP enumeration", "configure SMB shares", "test network services", or needs guidance on configuring and testing network services for penetration testing labs.
capabilities: Configure Apache HTTP and HTTPS servers with self-signed certificates
Set up SNMP service with community strings for enumeration practice
Configure Samba SMB shares with various permission levels
Use nmap, snmpwalk, enum4linux, and smbclient for network enumeration
Analyze HTTP and SMB logs for security insights
Create isolated lab environments for security testing
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate penetration testing education skill for setting up lab environments. Static findings mostly represent false positives (legitimate admin commands, test URLs). However, the skill configures vulnerable services (default SNMP communities, chmod 777) that pose real risks if used improperly. Requires warnings about authorized use only in isolated lab environments.
valueStatement: This skill helps security learners set up vulnerable network services in an isolated lab environment for hands-on penetration testing practice. It provides step-by-step instructions for configuring HTTP, HTTPS, SNMP, and SMB services with known configurations for safe testing.
limitations: Only for authorized, isolated lab environments - never use on production systems or unauthorized networks
Does not include actual exploitation techniques - only enumeration and setup
Self-signed certificates cause browser warnings and are not suitable for production
Default credentials configured in skill must never be used in production

useCases.title: Security Certification Prep
useCases.description: Security professionals preparing for CEH, OSCP, or CompTIA Security+ can use this skill to set up practice environments for enumeration techniques.

useCases.title: Cybersecurity Training Labs
useCases.description: Instructors can use this skill to quickly configure vulnerable target systems for hands-on training sessions.

useCases.title: Home Lab Security Practice
useCases.description: Learners can set up their own home labs to practice network reconnaissance and service enumeration safely.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Lab Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the network-101 skill to set up an HTTP server on port 80 for my penetration testing lab.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to configure a basic web server for testing

promptTemplates.title: SNMP Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure SNMP service with community strings for enumeration practice. Show me the commands to set up snmpd on Linux.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to set up SNMP for security testing

promptTemplates.title: SMB Share Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a Samba share with anonymous access for my lab environment. Include steps for configuring permissions.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to create SMB target for enumeration practice

promptTemplates.title: Enumeration Commands
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to enumerate the SMB service using enum4linux and smbmap. My target IP is 192.168.1.100.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to practice network enumeration techniques

bestPractices: Always use this skill in isolated, air-gapped lab environments separate from production networks
Document all changes made to lab systems and maintain separate credentials from production
Regularly rebuild lab systems to ensure clean, known states for testing
antiPatterns: Never apply configurations from this skill to production or internet-facing systems
Do not use default community strings (public/private) outside of isolated labs
Avoid leaving services enabled with chmod 777 permissions on any network

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: This skill is safe when used ONLY in isolated lab environments for authorized security testing. Never apply these configurations to production systems or networks you do not own.

faq.question: What tools does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers nmap for port scanning, snmpwalk for SNMP enumeration, smbclient and smbmap for SMB enumeration, and enum4linux for comprehensive SMB/NetBIOS enumeration.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for OSCP exam prep?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is excellent for OSCP and other penetration testing certification preparation. It helps you understand how services are configured and enumerated.

faq.question: Does this skill perform exploitation?
faq.answer: No, this skill only covers service configuration and enumeration techniques, not exploitation. It sets up vulnerable targets for safe practice.

faq.question: What operating systems are supported?
faq.answer: The skill covers both Linux (Apache, Snmpd, Samba) and Windows (IIS, Windows SNMP Service, Windows SMB) configurations.

faq.question: Why are default credentials used in this skill?
faq.answer: Default SNMP communities (public/private) and anonymous SMB access are configured intentionally for lab practice. These represent common misconfigurations that security testers encounter.
